name: Flix - La Granadella - Bellaguarda - Margalef - La Palma – circular route
name_original: Flix - La Granadella - Bellaguarda - Margalef - La Palma – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Flix navigate a landscape characterized by the Ebro River valley, offering a mix of riverside paths and ascents into surrounding hills. The region features varied terrain, from agricultural areas to more rugged, elevated sections. Road cyclists can expect routes that traverse open countryside and pass through small towns, with significant elevation changes on many of the longer paths. The area provides a diverse setting for road cycling, combining natural features with local infrastructure. Best road cycling routes around Flix The most popular road cycling route is Flix - La Granadella - Bellaguarda - Margalef - La Palma – circular route, a 48.5 miles (78.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 46 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 3,470 feet (1,058 meters) of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pantano de Riba-roja – circular from Riba-roja through Gandesa and Batea, a difficult 53.3 miles (85.8 km) path. This route includes over 4,070 feet (1,242 meters) of climbing, offering views of the Riba-roja reservoir and surrounding areas. Local road cyclists also love the Plaça Major Flix – Riba-roja Reservoir loop from Flix, a 48.3 miles (77.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the Riba-roja Reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around Flix is defined by river valleys, rolling hills, and open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, including challenging road cycling trails with significant climbs. The routes in Flix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Flix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Flix?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available around Flix on komoot, catering to various skill levels. This includes 2 easy, 27 moderate, and 28 difficult routes.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes for beginners in Flix?
A: Yes, Flix offers a few easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners. These routes typically have less elevation gain and shorter distances, providing a gentle introduction to the region's cycling. You can find 2 easy routes on komoot.

Q: What are the most challenging road cycling trails around Flix?
A: Flix is known for its challenging road cycling routes, with 28 difficult trails featuring significant climbs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966555278/flix-la-granadella-bellaguarda-margalef-la-palma-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flix - La Granadella - Bellaguarda - Margalef - La Palma – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, with over 3,470 feet (1,058 meters) of elevation gain, offer a demanding experience for experienced cyclists.

Q: What is the typical length of road cycling routes around Flix?
A: Road cycling routes around Flix vary significantly in length. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966555278/flix-la-granadella-bellaguarda-margalef-la-palma-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flix - La Granadella - Bellaguarda - Margalef - La Palma – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is 48.5 miles (78.0 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966545886/pantano-de-riba-roja-circular-from-riba-roja-through-gandesa-and-batea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Riba-roja – circular from Riba-roja through Gandesa and Batea&lt;/a&gt; extends to 53.3 miles (85.8 km).

Q: Are there any loop road bike trails in the Flix area?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Flix are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14893427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaça Major Flix – Riba-roja Reservoir loop from Flix&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38239693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Móra d'Ebre Bridge – Plaça Major Flix loop from Flix&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes near Flix?
A: Road cycling routes around Flix offer diverse scenery, characterized by the Ebro River valley, agricultural areas, and ascents into surrounding hills. You'll pass through open countryside and small towns, with views of natural features like the Riba-roja reservoir.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: While cycling around Flix, you can find several scenic viewpoints and natural attractions. Notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4584288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Priorat Balcony&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/845341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Fraguerau gorge&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3224462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clot del Cirer&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Flix?
A: The road cycling routes in Flix are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, challenging climbs, and scenic views of the Ebro River valley and surrounding areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Flix?
A: The Mediterranean climate of Flix generally offers pleasant conditions for road biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide mild temperatures and less extreme weather, making them ideal for longer rides. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended then.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls or gorges?
A: Yes, the region around Flix features natural attractions like gorges and waterfalls. While specific road cycling routes might not pass directly by them, you can find highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5014391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll de l'Ou Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/843527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cadolles Fondes Deep Pools&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the natural landscape and could be explored as detours or nearby points of interest.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that explore the Riba-roja Reservoir area?
A: Absolutely. The Riba-roja Reservoir is a prominent feature in the region, and several road cycling routes incorporate it. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966545886/pantano-de-riba-roja-circular-from-riba-roja-through-gandesa-and-batea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Riba-roja – circular from Riba-roja through Gandesa and Batea&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14893427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaça Major Flix – Riba-roja Reservoir loop from Flix&lt;/a&gt; are excellent options for exploring this area by bike.
tips: Good views of the town of La Granadella. The villa is located on a hill at an altitude of 528 meters, in the district of Les Garrigues, bordering the districts of Segrià and Ribera d'Ebre.

Near the town of La Bisbal de Falset is the cave of Santa Llúcia.

During the Civil War, the space became the hospital for the military units made up of republican volunteers who fought in the Battle of the Ebro.
